data.title: SouthState Corporation: Superior Profitability, Capital Strength, and Growth Outlook Support Buy Rating and Valuation Premium
data.description:
SouthState Corporation, the Financial sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Michael Rose from Raymond James reiterated a Buy rating on the stock and has a $120.00 price target.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Michael Rose has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors reflecting SouthState Corporation’s strong recent performance and favorable outlook. He highlights that the bank’s latest quarterly earnings surpassed both his own and market expectations, driven by solid loan and deposit growth that supported higher net interest income, as well as better-than-anticipated fee revenues from correspondent banking activities. In addition, credit quality remains sound, allowing for a lower loan loss provision, and tangible book value per share continued to grow. The company also accelerated capital return through share repurchases that were above prior forecasts, reinforcing confidence in management’s capital deployment strategy.
Rose’s positive stance is further supported by management’s guidance, which points to a stable and attractive net interest margin, manageable funding costs, and mid- to upper-single-digit loan growth supported by a strong pipeline, particularly in newer markets such as Texas and Colorado. Fee income is expected to normalize at healthy levels relative to assets, while expense growth should remain controlled even as the bank invests in technology and adds commercial relationship managers to support future growth. He views the bank’s profitability, capital strength, and credit profile as superior to peers, justifying a valuation premium and underpinning his increased price target. Together, these factors lead him to see an attractive risk-reward profile for SSB shares and to reiterate a Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $118.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Judges Scientific: Cyclical Headwinds but Strengthening Fundamentals Support Buy Rating
data.description:
Judges Scientific, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Vanessa Jeffriess from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a p7,380.00 price target.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Vanessa Jeffriess has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that balance near-term trading challenges against Judges Scientific’s structural strengths and self-help actions. While recent order trends have deteriorated, particularly in the US and offshore wind-linked demand, she views these pressures as cyclical rather than structural, with underlying demand in China and the rest of Europe holding up reasonably well. Jeffriess notes that management is already acting to right-size the cost base and protect margins as the order book has shortened, which should help earnings resilience while end markets remain weak. The lack of visibility on a quick recovery and a softer EPS outlook versus consensus are, in her view, already substantially reflected in the share price following prior weakness.
At the same time, Jeffriess sees scope for operational ‘progress’ as internal issues are contained and efficiencies are implemented, positioning the group to benefit disproportionately when demand normalizes. She considers Judges’ niche positions in scientific instrumentation, its track record of disciplined execution, and its diversified geographic exposure (outside the currently weak US) as key supports for the investment case. Although the M&A environment is currently difficult and Geotek’s slowdown is a drag, these factors are treated as temporary headwinds that do not undermine the company’s long-term growth potential. On this basis, she concludes that the risk‑reward profile has turned attractive, justifying a Buy recommendation despite near-term earnings pressure.

JDG’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from p8140.000 to p4820.000, which is a -40.79% drop .
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: IPST: High-Risk, High-Reward Equity Proxy on Story Protocol’s $IP Token with Undervalued Validator-Driven Upside
data.description:
Darren Aftahi, an analyst from Roth MKM, has initiated a new Buy rating on Heritage Distilling Holding Company (IPST).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Darren Aftahi has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that position Heritage Distilling Holding Company (IPST) as a leveraged, high-risk/high-reward proxy on the growth of Story Protocol’s $IP token and blockchain ecosystem. He views Story Protocol as a specialized infrastructure layer for making intellectual property and related assets programmable, enforceable, and easily licensable, which could be particularly valuable as AI models and datasets require stronger IP protection and monetization. The $IP token’s design, where network activity consumes tokens and partially removes them from circulation while rewarding validators, creates a direct linkage between real-world IP usage and token value. In Aftahi’s view, rising on-chain IP activity and enterprise adoption could therefore translate into both higher token prices and expanding economic value for IPST.

Aftahi also highlights IPST’s validator-focused business model, which generates high-margin, recurring yield from staking its treasury and earning commissions on delegated tokens, effectively creating a software-like, capital-efficient revenue stream. He notes that listed equity exposure to Story Protocol via IPST allows investors to participate in the upside of $IP without directly managing digital asset custody, while the company currently trades at a steep discount to the market value of its token holdings. His valuation framework combines the intrinsic value of IPST’s validator operations with a peer-based multiple on its net asset value, indicating that the shares are materially undervalued relative to comparable digital-asset treasury vehicles. Together, these elements underpin his conviction that IPST offers attractive upside potential, supporting his Buy rating and $8.20 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Babcock International: Management Continuity and Sustained Operating Momentum Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
Jefferies analyst David Farrell has maintained their bullish stance on BAB stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

David Farrell has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors centered on management continuity, strategic execution, and robust operating momentum. He views the planned retirement of CEO Sir David Lockwood at the end of 2026, together with the appointment of Harry Holt from the Nuclear division, as a well-managed transition that preserves the successful strategic direction established since 2020. Farrell emphasizes that Holt brings a track record of strong revenue and profit growth in Babcock’s largest business unit and is already well known to investors, which should reduce uncertainty and support confidence in the next phase of the company’s development.

Farrell also believes that Babcock has effectively moved beyond its turnaround phase and is entering a period where it can capitalize on a wider set of growth opportunities across all divisions, both in the UK and internationally. He highlights that the operational performance reported in the first half of fiscal 2026 has carried through into the third quarter, demonstrating sustained momentum rather than one-off improvement. Given this backdrop, Farrell sees potential for earnings in fiscal 2026 to exceed current market forecasts, reinforcing his positive stance on the shares and underpinning his Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Farrell is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 11.8% and a 69.51% success rate. Farrell covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Babcock International, Chemring, and FLSmidth & Co. A/S.

In another report released today, Berenberg Bank also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a p1,670.00 price target.
